concepts.title: Eutrophication
concepts.description: Eutrophication is the excessive growth of algae in water bodies due to high nutrient levels, leading to the depletion of dissolved oxygen and ultimately affecting aquatic life.

concepts.title: Biodegradable and Non-Biodegradable Wastes
concepts.description: Biodegradable wastes can decompose naturally, whereas non-biodegradable wastes do not decompose and cause environmental pollution.

concepts.title: Ozone Layer Depletion
concepts.description: The ozone layer depletion is mainly caused by the release of chlorofluorocarbons (CFCs), which damage the ozone layer, allowing harmful UV rays to reach the Earth's surface.

concepts.title: Three R's of Waste Management
concepts.description: Reduce, Reuse, Recycle (3Rs) - to minimize waste generation, use waste materials again, and convert waste into useful products, respectively.

concepts.title: Confusion between Biodiversity and Endangered Species
concepts.description: Biodiversity refers to the variety of life forms in an ecosystem, whereas endangered species are those that are threatened with extinction. Students often confuse these two terms, but they are not the same.

concepts.title: Pollution Index
concepts.description: The pollution index is a measure of the level of pollution in a particular area, with higher values indicating greater pollution. Students should remember the different types of pollution (air, water, soil) and their effects on human health and the environment.

concepts.title: Acid Rain
concepts.description: Acid rain is caused by the release of pollutants such as sulfur dioxide and nitrogen oxides into the atmosphere, which combine with water and oxygen to form acidic compounds that can harm plants, animals, and infrastructure.

concepts.title: Population Explosion
concepts.description: The rapid increase in human population is a major environmental issue, leading to increased pressure on resources, loss of biodiversity, and degradation of the environment.
